title: alnus incana f. monstrosa
intro: Alnus incana f. monstrosa, also known as the white green alder or giant green alder, is a rare, deciduous tree cultivar of the green alder tree species (Alnus incana). It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, as well as its potential health benefits in reducing blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health. The plant's bark, leaves, and cones can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Alnus incana f. monstrosa for medicinal purposes.
